---
title: "Generating F2 c-h"
output: html_document
date: "2026-04-30"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(brms)   
library(ggpubr)
library(tidybayes)
library(cowplot)
library(ggtext)
library(wesanderson)
library(broom.mixed) 
library(posterior)
key <- read_csv('../data/food_group_color_key_final.csv', col_types = 'ccccc')
palette <- wes_palette("Royal1", 2)

axis_text_size <- 10
axis_title_size <- 10
```

## fg model

```{r}
data <- read_csv("../data/153_combined_META.csv") %>% 
  mutate(intensity = factor(intensity, levels = c('nonablative','reduced','ablative'))) %>%
  mutate(pid = factor(pid)) %>%
    # This divides all the new intake columns by 100.
  mutate(across(c(starts_with("fg_"), starts_with("ave_")), ~ .x / 100))

all_food_vars <- data %>% select(starts_with('fg')) %>% colnames()

# Create the interaction terms for all food variables in this iteration
interaction_terms <- paste(all_food_vars, "empirical", sep = "*")

# Build the full formula string dynamically
formula_string <- paste(
  "log(simpson_reciprocal) ~ 0 + intensity + empirical + TPN + EN  +",
  paste(interaction_terms, collapse = " + "),
  "+ (1 | pid) + (1 | timebin)"
)

# Convert the string to a formula object
formula <- brms::bf(as.formula(formula_string))

# Build the priors by adding them together.
# This single prior() call applies to all coefficients listed in `all_food_coefs`.
priors <-
    prior(normal(0, 1), class = 'b') + # General prior for all food effects
    # Specific priors that override the general one for non-food covariates
    prior(normal(0, 0.1), class = 'b', coef = "TPNTRUE") +
    prior(normal(0, 0.1), class = 'b', coef = "ENTRUE") +
    prior(normal(0, 0.5), class = 'b', coef = "empiricalTRUE") +
    prior(normal(2, .1), class = 'b', coef = "intensityablative") +
    prior(normal(2, .1), class = 'b', coef = "intensityreduced") +
    prior(normal(2, .1), class = 'b', coef = "intensitynonablative")

#Fit the model (using fewer iterations for this example to run quickly)
model_fit <- brm(
  formula = formula,
  data = data,
  prior = priors,
    warmup = 1000, iter = 3000,
    chains = 4, cores = 4,
    seed = 123,
    silent = 2,
  control = list(adapt_delta = 0.99)
)

results_df <- tidy(model_fit, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(conf.low = round(conf.low, 2),
         conf.high = round(conf.high, 2)) |> 
  mutate(
    n_samples = nrow(data),
    n_patients = n_distinct(data$pid),
  )

results_df |> write_csv('../data/172_results_df_main_fg_diversity.csv')
```


## test the difference 

```{r}
# # Create the interaction terms for all food variables in this iteration
# interaction_terms_ <- paste(all_food_vars, "empirical", sep = "*")
# 
# # Build the full formula string dynamically
# formula_string_ <- paste(
#   "log(simpson_reciprocal) ~ 0 + intensity + empirical + TPN + EN  +",
#   paste(interaction_terms_, collapse = " + "),
#   "+ (1 | pid) + (1 | timebin)"
# )
# 
# # Convert the string to a formula object
# formula_ <- brms::bf(as.formula(formula_string_))
# 
# 
# #Fit the model (using fewer iterations for this example to run quickly)
# model_fit_ <- brm(
#   formula = formula_,
#   data = data,
#   prior = priors,
#     warmup = 1000, iter = 3000,
#     chains = 4, cores = 4,
#     seed = 123,
#     silent = 2,
#   control = list(adapt_delta = 0.99)
# )
# 
# results_df_star <- tidy(model_fit_, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
#   mutate(conf.low = round(conf.low, 2),
#          conf.high = round(conf.high, 2)) |> 
#   mutate(
#     n_samples = nrow(data),
#     n_patients = n_distinct(data$pid),
#   )
```
### comparison

```{r}
# # Read in the datasets. The second filename is a placeholder and should be updated.
# df1 <- read_csv("../data/172_results_df_main_fg_diversity.csv")
# df2 <- results_df_star
# 
# # Filter for fixed effects and grab the estimate and confidence intervals for the first model
# model1_fixed <- df1 |>
#   filter(effect == "fixed") |>
#   select(term, estimate_1 = estimate, conf_low_1 = conf.low, conf_high_1 = conf.high)
# 
# # Do the exact same extraction for the second model
# model2_fixed <- df2 |>
#   filter(effect == "fixed") |>
#   select(term, estimate_2 = estimate, conf_low_2 = conf.low, conf_high_2 = conf.high)
# 
# # Merge the two datasets side-by-side based on the term names
# comparison_table <- model1_fixed |>
#   full_join(model2_fixed, by = "term")
# 
# # Print the side-by-side table
# 
# # Visualizing the differences is often much clearer than looking at raw numbers.
# # Binding the rows together with an identifier makes it easy to plot.
# plot_data <- bind_rows(
#   df1 |> filter(effect == "fixed") |> mutate(model = "Model 1"),
#   df2 |> filter(effect == "fixed") |> mutate(model = "Model 2 original")
# )
# 
# # Create a forest plot to see if the confidence intervals overlap
# ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = estimate, y = term, color = model)) +
#   geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = 0.5), size = 2) +
#   geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high), 
#                  position = position_dodge(width = 0.5), 
#                  height = 0.3) +
#   theme_minimal() +
#   labs(title = "Fixed Effects Comparison",
#        x = "Estimate (with 95% CI)",
#        y = "Model Term")
```



```{r}
# Get all draws as a data frame (one row per draw)
draws <- as_draws_df(model_fit)

# Extract draws as a data frame
write_csv(draws, "../data/172_div_fg_posterior_draws.csv")
```

## 2d

```{r}
replacement_dictionary <- setNames(key$shortname, key$fg1_name)

level_order <- rev(c(
    "abx", "EN", "TPN", 
    "abx * Sweets", "Sweets",
    "abx * Grains", "Grains",
    "abx * Milk", "Milk",
    "abx * Eggs", "Eggs",
    "abx * Legumes", "Legumes",
    "abx * Meats", "Meats",
    "abx * Fruits", "Fruits",
    "abx * Oils", "Oils",
    "abx * Vegetables", "Vegetables"
))

cleaned_effects <- results_df %>%
  # Keep only the fixed effects
  filter(effect == "fixed") %>%
  # Create a new column to distinguish main effects from interactions
  mutate(
    effect_type = if_else(str_detect(term, ":"), "Interaction", "Main Effect"),
    # Create clean labels for plotting
    clean_term = term %>%
      str_replace("empiricalTRUE$", "abx") %>%
      str_remove_all("empiricalFALSE:|avg_intake_|TRUE$") %>%
      str_replace_all( replacement_dictionary) %>%
      str_replace("empiricalTRUE:", "abx * ") %>%
      str_replace_all("_", " ") 
  ) %>%
  filter(!str_detect(clean_term, 'intensity')) %>%
  # Create a new column to identify significant results
  # The condition is TRUE if conf.low and conf.high have the same sign (i.e., don't cross zero)
  mutate(is_significant = (conf.low * conf.high) >= 0) %>%
  mutate(clean_term = factor(clean_term, levels = level_order))

shading_df <- cleaned_effects %>%
  mutate(y_numeric = as.numeric(clean_term)) %>%
  filter(str_detect(clean_term, "\\*")) # Filter for interaction terms

plot_ <- ggplot(cleaned_effects, aes(x = estimate, y = clean_term)) +
  geom_rect(
    data = shading_df,
    aes(ymin = y_numeric - 0.5, ymax = y_numeric + 0.5, xmin = -Inf, xmax = Inf),
    fill = "#FBEADC", # A light orange/peach color like the example
    alpha = 0.7,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
   ) +
   # Add a vertical line at zero, which represents "no effect"
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "solid", color = "blue",size = 0.8) +
   # Use geom_pointrange to show the estimate (point) and confidence interval (line)
   geom_pointrange(
   aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high, color = is_significant),
     size = 0.25,linewidth = 1,
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "TRUE" = "red",
      "FALSE" = "black"
    ), name = "Effect Status" # Legend title
  ) +
  scale_y_discrete( labels = function(x) { ifelse(str_detect(x, '\\*'),  str_glue("<b style='color:royalblue'>{x}</b>"), as.character(x)) }) +
  # Add labels and a clean theme
  labs(
    x = "ln(diversity) change",
    y = ''
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  theme(legend.position = "none", axis.text.y = element_markdown(), aspect.ratio = 1.3) 

plot_
```

## 2c

```{r}
intercepts <- results_df %>% 
  filter(str_detect(term, "intensity")) |> 
  mutate(
    shortname  = str_replace(term, 'intensity',''),
    shortname = str_to_title(shortname),
    shortname = factor(shortname, levels = c('Nonablative','Reduced','Ablative'))) %>% 
  
  ggplot( aes(x = estimate, y = shortname)) +
  geom_pointrange(
   aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high),
     size = 0.25,linewidth = 1,
  ) +
  labs(x = 'ln(diversity)', y = '') +
   xlim(1.5, 2.5) +
  scale_x_continuous( breaks = seq(1.4, 2.4, 0.2)) + 
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position = 'none') +
  theme(axis.text = element_text( size = 10),
          panel.background = element_rect(fill = "gray96",colour = "gray96",size = 0.5, linetype = "solid"),
        axis.title=element_text(size=10), 
        aspect.ratio=1.4)

intercepts
```
## 2g

```{r}
# Pulling the conditional effects directly from the model to build the smooth curves
raw_conditional <- conditional_effects(model_fit, surface = TRUE)

# Filtering down to just the interactions between food groups and antibiotics

condi_dat <- raw_conditional[str_detect(names(raw_conditional), ":empirical")] |> 
  bind_rows(.id = 'grp') |> 
  mutate(grp = str_replace(grp, '\\:empirical','')) |> 
  mutate(
    # Stretching the x-axis back to normal grams for readability on the final plot
    effect1__ = effect1__ * 100,
    across(starts_with("fg_"), ~ .x * 100) 
  ) |> 
  left_join(key |> select(grp = fg1_name, shortname)) %>% 
  mutate(shortname = factor(shortname, levels = rev( c('Vegetables',
                 'Oils',
                 'Fruits',
                 'Meats',
                 'Legumes',
                 'Eggs',
                 'Milk',
                 'Grains',
                 'Sweets'))))
```


```{r}
# Generating the prediction curves (Panels G/H)
conditional_plots <- condi_dat |>   
  ggplot() +
  geom_smooth(
    aes(x = effect1__, y = estimate__, ymin = lower__, ymax = upper__, fill = effect2__, color = effect2__),
    stat = "identity", alpha = .3, linewidth = 1.5
  ) + 
    scale_fill_manual('antibiotics', values = palette, labels=c('not exposed', 'exposed')) +
  scale_colour_manual('antibiotics', values = palette, labels=c('not exposed', 'exposed')) +
 facet_wrap(~ shortname, nrow = 3, scales = 'free_x') +
  ylim(0, 3.3) +
  labs(y = 'Predicted log(diversity)', x = 'Food group consumed (grams)') +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position = 'none', legend.title = element_text(size = 8, face="bold"),
        legend.text = element_text( size=8, face="bold"),
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#FAEFD140"),
        legend.background=element_rect(fill = alpha("white", 0)),
       legend.key=element_rect(fill = alpha("white", 0)),
        aspect.ratio = 1/1.5, 
        strip.background = element_rect(color="white", fill='#ffecdc', size=1.5,linetype="solid"),
     axis.text=element_text(size=8), axis.title=element_text(size=axis_text_size))    +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(direction = "vertical"), color = guide_legend(direction = "vertical"))

conditional_plots
```

# macronutrients

```{r}
# Define the variables of interest
macro_vars <- c("ave_Fat_g", "ave_Fibers_g", "ave_Sugars_g")

# Build interaction terms connecting macronutrients with empirical antibiotic use
interaction_terms_macro <- paste(macro_vars, "empirical", sep = "*")

formula_string_macro <- paste(
  "log(simpson_reciprocal) ~ 0 + intensity + empirical + TPN + EN  +",
  paste(interaction_terms_macro, collapse = " + "),
  "+ (1 | pid) + (1 | timebin)"
)

formula_macro <- brms::bf(as.formula(formula_string_macro))

# Priors keep the model grounded. The general prior applies to the macro effects.
priors_macro <-
    prior(normal(0, 1), class = 'b') + 
    prior(normal(0, 0.1), class = 'b', coef = "TPNTRUE") +
    prior(normal(0, 0.1), class = 'b', coef = "ENTRUE") +
    prior(normal(0, 0.5), class = 'b', coef = "empiricalTRUE") +
    prior(normal(2, .1), class = 'b', coef = "intensityablative") +
    prior(normal(2, .1), class = 'b', coef = "intensityreduced") +
    prior(normal(2, .1), class = 'b', coef = "intensitynonablative")

model_fit_macro <- brm(
  formula = formula_macro,
  data = data,
  prior = priors_macro,
  warmup = 1000, iter = 3000,
  chains = 4, cores = 4,
  seed = 123,
  silent = 2,
  control = list(adapt_delta = 0.99)
)

results_df_macro <- tidy(model_fit_macro, conf.int = TRUE) |>
  mutate(conf.low = round(conf.low, 2),
         conf.high = round(conf.high, 2)) |> 
  mutate(
    n_samples = nrow(data),
    n_patients = n_distinct(data$pid),
  )

results_df_macro |> write_csv('../data/172_results_df_macro_diversity.csv')
```

## 2e

```{r}
# Set up a dictionary to clean up the macronutrient names for plotting
macro_dict <- c(
  "ave_Fat_g" = "Fat", 
  "ave_Fibers_g" = "Fiber", 
  "ave_Sugars_g" = "Sugars"
)

level_order_macro <- rev(c(
    "abx", "EN", "TPN", 
    "abx * Sugars", "Sugars",
    "abx * Fiber", "Fiber",
    "abx * Fat", "Fat"
))

cleaned_effects_macro <- results_df_macro |>
  filter(effect == "fixed") |>
  mutate(
    effect_type = if_else(str_detect(term, ":"), "Interaction", "Main Effect"),
    clean_term = term |>
      str_replace("empiricalTRUE$", "abx") |>
      str_remove_all("empiricalFALSE:|TRUE$") |>
      str_replace_all(macro_dict) |>
      str_replace("empiricalTRUE:", "abx * ") |>
      str_replace_all("_", " ") 
  ) |>
  filter(!str_detect(clean_term, 'intensity')) |>
  # Determine significance if confidence bounds don't cross zero
  mutate(is_significant = (conf.low * conf.high) >= 0) |>
  mutate(clean_term = factor(clean_term, levels = level_order_macro))

shading_df_macro <- cleaned_effects_macro |>
  mutate(y_numeric = as.numeric(clean_term)) |>
  filter(str_detect(clean_term, "\\*")) 

plot_macro <- ggplot(cleaned_effects_macro, aes(x = estimate, y = clean_term)) +
  geom_rect(
    data = shading_df_macro,
    aes(ymin = y_numeric - 0.5, ymax = y_numeric + 0.5, xmin = -Inf, xmax = Inf),
    fill = "#6B8E2340", 
    alpha = 0.3,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
   ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "solid", color = "blue", size = 0.8) +
  geom_pointrange(
   aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high, color = is_significant),
     size = 0.25, linewidth = 1,
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("TRUE" = "red", "FALSE" = "black"), 
    name = "Effect Status" 
  ) +
  scale_y_discrete(labels = function(x) { ifelse(str_detect(x, '\\*'),  str_glue("<b style='color:royalblue'>{x}</b>"), as.character(x)) }) +
  labs(x = "ln(diversity) change", y = '') +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  theme(legend.position = "none", axis.text.y = element_markdown(), aspect.ratio = 1.3) 

plot_macro
```

## 2h

```{r}
raw_conditional_macro <- conditional_effects(model_fit_macro, surface = TRUE)

condi_dat_macro <- raw_conditional_macro[str_detect(names(raw_conditional_macro), ":empirical")] |> 
  bind_rows(.id = 'grp') |> 
  mutate(grp = str_replace(grp, '\\:empirical','')) |> 
  mutate(
    # Re-scale back to real-world units (grams) to make the plots readable
    effect1__ = effect1__ * 100,
    across(starts_with("ave_"), ~ .x * 100) 
  ) |> 
  # Apply the macro dictionary to translate variable names to plot labels
  mutate(shortname = recode(grp, !!!macro_dict)) |> 
  mutate(shortname = factor(shortname))

conditional_plots_macro <- condi_dat_macro |>   
  ggplot() +
  geom_smooth(
    aes(x = effect1__, y = estimate__, ymin = lower__, ymax = upper__, fill = effect2__, color = effect2__),
    stat = "identity", alpha = .3, linewidth = 1.5
  ) + 
  scale_fill_manual('antibiotics', values = palette, labels=c('not exposed', 'exposed')) +
  scale_colour_manual('antibiotics', values = palette, labels=c('not exposed', 'exposed')) +
  facet_wrap(~ shortname, nrow = 1, scales = 'free_x') +
  ylim(0, 3.3) +
  labs(y = 'Predicted log(diversity)', x = 'Macronutrient consumed (grams)') +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    legend.position = 'none', 
    legend.title = element_text(size = 8, face="bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size=8, face="bold"),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#6B8E2320"),
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = alpha("white", 0)),
    legend.key = element_rect(fill = alpha("white", 0)),
    aspect.ratio = 1/1.5, 
    strip.background = element_rect(color="white", fill='#6B8E2340', size=1.5, linetype="solid"),
    axis.text = element_text(size=8), 
    axis.title = element_text(size=10)
  ) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(direction = "vertical"), color = guide_legend(direction = "vertical"))

conditional_plots_macro

```

## raw scatter

```{r}
# making a df for annotating the number of samples and patients in the two groups
META <- read_csv('../data/153_combined_META.csv')  

num_annot <- META %>% 
  split(.$empirical) %>% 
  imap_dfr(function(df, name_) {
    df %>% distinct(pid) %>% nrow
  }) %>% gather('empirical','pt_num') %>% 
  full_join(META %>% count(empirical) %>% mutate(empirical = as.character(empirical))) %>% 
  rename(sample_num = n) %>% 
  mutate(antibiotics = if_else(empirical == 'FALSE', 'not exposed', 'exposed')) %>% 
  mutate(lbl = str_glue('{sample_num} samples\nfrom {pt_num} patients'))

# the raw data 
rawsweets <- META %>% 
  mutate(log_div = log(simpson_reciprocal)) %>% 
  mutate(antibiotics = if_else(empirical == 'FALSE', 'not exposed', 'exposed'),
         antibiotics = factor(antibiotics, levels = c('not exposed','exposed'))) %>% 
  ggscatter(x = 'fg_sweets', y = 'log_div', color = 'antibiotics',
            ylab = 'ln(diversity)',xlab = 'Sweets consumed (grams)',
            alpha = 0.35, shape = 16, size = 1,
            add = "reg.line",  # Add regressin line
           add.params = list(color = 'antibiotics', fill = 'antibiotics', alpha = 0.3, size = 1.5), # Customize line
           conf.int = TRUE, # Add confidence interval
           cor.coef = TRUE, # Add correlation coefficient.
           cor.coeff.args = list(method = "spearman",  label.sep = "\n", cor.coef.name = c("rho"),p.accuracy = 0.01, r.accuracy = 0.01,label.x.npc = "middle", label.y.npc = "top", size = 3.5)) +
  scale_x_sqrt() +
  scale_y_sqrt() +
  scale_fill_manual('antibiotics', values = palette, labels=c('not exposed', 'exposed')) +
  scale_colour_manual('antibiotics',values = palette, labels=c('not exposed', 'exposed')) +
  facet_wrap(~ antibiotics, labeller = 'label_both', dir = 'h') +
  theme(aspect.ratio = 1/1.15, 
        legend.position = 'none',
          strip.background = element_blank(),
        strip.text.x = element_blank(), 
     axis.text=element_text(size=9), axis.title=element_text(size=9)) 
rawsweets 
```

```{r}
# a version of the above with only sdrt < 0 samples 
META <- read_csv('../data/153_combined_META.csv')  |>
  filter(sdrt < 0)

num_annot <- META %>% 
  split(.$empirical) %>% 
  imap_dfr(function(df, name_) {
    df %>% distinct(pid) %>% nrow
  }) %>% gather('empirical','pt_num') %>% 
  full_join(META %>% count(empirical) %>% mutate(empirical = as.character(empirical))) %>% 
  rename(sample_num = n) %>% 
  mutate(antibiotics = if_else(empirical == 'FALSE', 'not exposed', 'exposed')) %>% 
  mutate(lbl = str_glue('{sample_num} samples\nfrom {pt_num} patients'))

# the raw data 
rawsweets_pre <- META %>% 
  mutate(log_div = log(simpson_reciprocal)) %>% 
  mutate(antibiotics = if_else(empirical == 'FALSE', 'not exposed', 'exposed'),
         antibiotics = factor(antibiotics, levels = c('not exposed','exposed'))) %>% 
  ggscatter(x = 'fg_sweets', y = 'log_div', color = 'antibiotics',
            ylab = 'ln(diversity)',xlab = 'Sweets consumed (grams)',
            alpha = 0.35, shape = 16, size = 1,
            add = "reg.line",  # Add regressin line
           add.params = list(color = 'antibiotics', fill = 'antibiotics', alpha = 0.3, size = 1.5), # Customize line
           conf.int = TRUE, # Add confidence interval
           cor.coef = TRUE, # Add correlation coefficient.
           cor.coeff.args = list(method = "spearman",  label.sep = "\n", cor.coef.name = c("rho"),p.accuracy = 0.01, r.accuracy = 0.01,label.x.npc = "middle", label.y.npc = "top", size = 3.5)) +
    geom_text(data = num_annot, aes(label = lbl), 
              x = Inf, y = Inf, hjust = 0.6, vjust = 1.5) +
  scale_x_sqrt() +
  scale_y_sqrt() +
  scale_fill_manual('antibiotics', values = palette, labels=c('not exposed', 'exposed')) +
  scale_colour_manual('antibiotics',values = palette, labels=c('not exposed', 'exposed')) +
  facet_wrap(~ antibiotics, labeller = 'label_both', dir = 'h') +
  theme(aspect.ratio = 1/1.15, 
        legend.position = 'none',
          strip.background = element_blank(),
        strip.text.x = element_blank(), 
     axis.text=element_text(size=9), axis.title=element_text(size=9)) 
rawsweets_pre
```
## effect sizes for the abx * sweets on the original scale 

```{r}
fg_results <- read_csv("../data/172_results_df_main_fg_diversity.csv")

sweets_interaction <- fg_results |>
  filter(term == "empiricalTRUE:fg_sweets") |>
  # exp() turns a log-scale difference into a multiplicative factor;
  # 1 minus that factor is the proportional reduction in diversity
  mutate(
    pct_change      = (1 - exp(estimate))  * 100,
    pct_change_low  = (1 - exp(conf.high)) * 100,  # less negative bound -> smaller reduction
    pct_change_high = (1 - exp(conf.low))  * 100   # more negative bound -> larger reduction
  ) |>
  # keep the reported figures to one decimal place
  mutate(across(starts_with("pct_change"), ~ round(.x, 1))) |>
  select(term, estimate, conf.low, conf.high,
         pct_change, pct_change_low, pct_change_high)

sweets_interaction
```


```{r}
macro_results <- read_csv("../data/172_results_df_macro_diversity.csv")

sugars_interaction <- macro_results |>
  filter(term == "empiricalTRUE:ave_Sugars_g") |>
  # exp() turns a log-scale difference into a multiplicative factor;
  # 1 minus that factor is the proportional reduction in diversity
  mutate(
    pct_change      = (1 - exp(estimate))  * 100,
    pct_change_low  = (1 - exp(conf.high)) * 100,  # less negative bound -> smaller reduction
    pct_change_high = (1 - exp(conf.low))  * 100   # more negative bound -> larger reduction
  ) |>
  # keep the reported figures to one decimal place
  mutate(across(starts_with("pct_change"), ~ round(.x, 1))) |>
  select(term, estimate, conf.low, conf.high,
         pct_change, pct_change_low, pct_change_high)

sugars_interaction
```


```{r}
combined <- plot_grid(
  intercepts, plot_, plot_macro,conditional_plots, conditional_plots_macro
)

ggsave(
  '../data/F2c_172_.pdf', 
  plot = intercepts, 
  width = 90, 
  height = 90, 
  units = "mm", 
  device = 'pdf'
)


ggsave(
  '../data/F2d_172_.pdf', 
  plot = plot_, 
  width = 90, 
  height = 140, 
  units = "mm", 
  device = 'pdf'
)


ggsave(
  '../data/F2e_172_.pdf', 
  plot = plot_macro, 
  width = 90, 
  height = 140, 
  units = "mm", 
  device = 'pdf'
)

ggsave(
  '../data/F2g_172_.pdf', 
  plot = conditional_plots, 
  width = 90, 
  height = 140, 
  units = "mm", 
  device = 'pdf'
)

ggsave(
  '../data/F2h_172_.pdf', 
  plot = conditional_plots_macro, 
  width = 90, 
  height = 140, 
  units = "mm", 
  device = 'pdf'
)

ggsave(
  '../data/F2f_172_.pdf', 
  plot = rawsweets, 
  width = 90, 
  height = 140, 
  units = "mm", 
  device = 'pdf'
)

ggsave(
  '../data/E05i_172_.pdf', 
  plot = rawsweets_pre, 
  width = 90, 
  height = 140, 
  units = "mm", 
  device = 'pdf'
)
```
